uplink.description: typical fair-weather reading (1013 hPa, 25 C, 55%, wind 4.47 m/s @ 180 deg)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds weather frame; expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js (parseWeather) and converting upstream string values to numbers under the documented vocabulary mapping. Wind direction 180 deg is unchanged by the 360->0 wrap.

uplink.description: cold high-humidity calm (965 hPa, 0 C, 90%, no wind, direction 359 deg)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds weather frame; expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js. Direction 359 is the largest value left untouched by the 360->0 wrap; pressure stays above the 900 hPa atmospheric floor.

uplink.description: hot stormy high-wind (1056 hPa, 43.33 C, 20%, wind 20.12 m/s, direction raw 360 -> 0)
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds weather frame; expected.data derived by running reference/upstream-codec.js. Davis reports North as raw 360; our codec wraps it to 0 so wind.direction stays strictly below the vocabulary's exclusiveMaximum of 360 (upstream emits 360, which would be out of bounds).

uplink.description: wrong report kind (0x0B sub-type 2 but kind 0x01 = particulate, not weather)
uplink._note: Synthetic negative case: a valid MCF88 report-data header (0x0B, sub-type 2) but report kind 0x01 (particulate matter), which this weather codec does not handle.
